title: Greater Manchester Police gets top grade in new quality rating

input.lead: The new ranking comes four years after the force was taken out of special measures
input.summary: The new ranking comes four years after the force was taken out of special measures
input.evidence_text: Greater Manchester Police gets top grade in new quality rating - BBC News Image source, PA Media Image caption, Greater Manchester Police was placed in special measures in 2020 By Dan Wareing North West Published 1 hour ago Greater Manchester Police has been given the highest possible rating in a new national quality ranking system, four years after it was taken out of special measures. The force has been awarded Level 1 status in the Home Office's Police Performance System, which was introduced by the government in April. The rating means it requires no action or intervention. GMP said the outcome was "a source of considerable encouragement and one in which every one of its members can take legitimate pride". Chief Constable Sir Stephen Watson said: "Whilst this is an occasion upon which we should reflect with some satisfaction, we should also recognise that performance is not a destination but a continuing endeavour. "The communities we serve rightly expect us to strive constantly for improvement and we remain ambitious and restless in that pursuit." In 2020, the force was put into special measures after a report found it had failed to record 80,000 crimes in a year. The matter led to the resignation of the former chief constable. Two years later it was taken out of the measures, and then the following year the watchdog found the force had significantly improved . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230. Related topics Greater Manchester Police Manchester Force has significantly improved - inspectorate Publi

input.summary: Marwan Jumaa, who is serving a sentence for attacking two women, escaped on Tuesday evening
input.evidence_text: Police have arrested a prisoner who escaped during a transfer from a secure hospital facility to North Manchester Hospital on Tuesday evening Marwan Jumaa, 20, is in Lancashire Constabulary custody following a manhunt, Greater Manchester Police said in the early hours of Friday "An observant Lancashire Constabulary officer recognised Jumaa and acted swiftly to arrest him," Detective Supt Nicola McCulloch said in a statement. "He will now be returned to a secure hospital facility." Officers had issued an appeal for information after Jumaa, who is serving an indefinite sentence for attacking two women, absconded during the hospital transfer. He was described as a risk to others and himself The public had been warned to not approach him, but were instead urged to contact authorities McCulloch continued: "This has been a fast-paced investigation which has involved a significant number of officers and resources from Greater Manchester Police

title: Fan may get 'only joking' added to England World Cup winners tattoo after defeat

input.lead: Sean Carrington says he has "no regrets" about his tattoo despite the Three Lions' frustrating exit
input.summary: Sean Carrington says he has "no regrets" about his tattoo despite the Three Lions' frustrating exit
input.evidence_text: An England fan who was so convinced the Three Lions would win the World Cup that he got a huge tattoo celebrating the "win" has decided to add the words "only joking" underneath Sean Carrington got the foot-long £550 inking of the trophy with the words "England World Cup Winner 2026" written below it after the team beat DR Congo But along with millions of other supporters he was left railing at another frustrating England exit after Wednesday night's late defeat against Argentina in the semi-final The 36-year-old, from Wigan, Greater Manchester, said he had "no regrets" about having it done and was now considering how to have it amended However, he admitted he was pleased he rowed back on his original plan - which was to get "It's Coming Home" emblazoned across his chest Sean watched the match at Manchester's Depot Mayfield fanzone and said the mood was hopeful when England took the lead with Anthony Gordon's neat strike in the 55th minute

title: Brain injury epidemic coming due to headers, warns Nobby Stiles' son

input.lead: John Stiles says women's football is "a particular concern" after an inquest heard headers had contributed to his father's brain disease
input.summary: John Stiles says women's football is "a particular concern" after an inquest heard headers had contributed to his father's brain disease
input.evidence_text: The son of England's 1966 World Cup winner Nobby Stiles is predicting an "epidemic" of brain injuries among players who head footballs, especially women John Stiles spoke after an inquest on Wednesday into the death of his father, Nobby Stiles - the 78-year-old ex-Man Utd and England midfielder - heard thousands of headers during his career had caused his chronic traumatic encephalopathy (CTE) Senior Coroner Alison Mulch is writing a prevention of future deaths report after recording Stiles' cause of death in 2020 as Alzheimer's disease, contributed to by CTE The Football Association (FA), which has been asked to comment, is phasing out all football heading up to under-11s this year Stiles died with severe dementia in 2020 aged 78 and had headed a football about 140,000 times in his career, Stockport Coroners' Court heard during the inquest into his death, held on Wednesday Neuropathology expert Dr Daniel Du Plessis told the court: "I'm quite convinced his heading the football that many times has caused his CTE [chronic traumatic encephalopathy]."

title: Rochdale grooming gang leader removed from Accrington, says MP

input.lead: A Lancashire MP says she is "disgusted" paedophile Shabir Ahmed was placed into "our community"
input.summary: A Lancashire MP says she is "disgusted" paedophile Shabir Ahmed was placed into "our community"
input.evidence_text: Rochdale grooming gang leader removed from Accrington, says MP - BBC News Image source, Police handout Image caption, An MP said it was "deeply shocking" Shabir Ahmed was "released into our community" By Lynette Horsburgh North West Published 16 July 2026 A Rochdale grooming gang leader has been removed from a town where he was placed after his release from prison, following concerns he had been housed near a playground and a mosque. Shabir Ahmed, who was jailed for multiple counts of child rape and sexual offences in 2012, was placed in Accrington after being released on licence at the start of July. In a letter to Lancashire Police, Kamran Mahmood, the chief executive of Ghausia Rizvia Jamia Masjid, said putting Ahmed nearby was "a serious error of judgement" and threatened "public safety" and "community cohesion". Hyndburn MP Sarah Smith said he had now been moved but she was "disgusted that he was ever here in the first place". The Ministry of Justice confirmed to the BBC Ahmed had been relocated outside the borough. Ahmed, who came to the UK from Pakistan in the late 1960s, was one of nine men from Rochdale and Oldham who were convicted of exploiting, abusing and raping girls as young as 12 at two takeaway restaurants. He was jailed for 22 years and released on licence earlier this month. He had dual British-Pakistani citizenship before being stripped of his UK passport following his conviction but cannot be deported due to a 1971 law forbidding the removal of a small group of Commonwealth citizens who arrived in the UK more than 50 years ago. The Home Office said the provisions of the 1971 act had "protected many individuals caught up in the Windrush crisis", but Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood has announced plans to change the law following mounting calls for Ahme

title: The man who balances bikes, ladders and sheds on his chin

input.lead: Salford builder Tobey Newall plans to set the world record for balancing large items on his chin
input.summary: Salford builder Tobey Newall plans to set the world record for balancing large items on his chin
input.evidence_text: A builder who can balance items like sheds, ladders and bicycles on his chin is hoping to break a world record Tobey Newall, 25, from Salford, said he had discovered the talent after a trip to a DIY store with his brother six years ago when he tried to balance a plank of timber on his face "I don't know what possessed me to put it on my chin but I just did it and ended up being quite good at it," he told BBC Radio Manchester Newall, who goes by the nickname Chin-A-Rooney on social media, wants to break the Guinness World Record for the tallest object balanced on a chin, a title set by US-based juggler Cardon Firth in 2018 for balancing a 71ft (21m) object The objects Newall balances come in all shapes and sizes, including scaffolding boards and poles, sheeting material, and a 10ft (3m) worktop weighing 101lbs (46kg) He has about 350,000 followers and has clocked up millions of views for the videos of his chin-balancing feats

title: Why voting method change matters in mayoral poll

input.lead: Why the Supplementary Vote may allow voters to vote with their heads and their hearts
input.summary: Why the Supplementary Vote may allow voters to vote with their heads and their hearts
input.evidence_text: The Labour Party could benefit most from the return of the Supplementary Votes (SV) system in the Greater Manchester mayoral race, a polling expert has said Voters in the region head to the polls on 30 July and will be able to select a first and second choice of candidate as SV comes into replace the first-past-the-post (FPTP) system used at the last election A number of candidates have already declared for the contest , triggered after Andy Burnham's successful campaign to become the MP of Makerfield, leaving the post he has held since 2017 vacant Prof Jonathan Tonge of the University of Liverpool said Labour could pick up second preference votes from Greens and Lib Dems, while Reform could be less likely to see Restore voters put them as second preference due to "bad blood" between the party leaders The system was used in the first two mayoral elections, but was scrapped by the last Conservative government in favour of FPTP system, before it was reintroduced again by Labour Under SCV, candidates need to win more than 50% of the vote in the first round to be come mayor or the top two candidates go into a run-off where second preference votes from eliminated candidates are counted

title: What is the Greater Manchester mayor and what do they do?

input.lead: Greater Manchester goes to the polls on 30 July, but what powers does the region's mayor hold?
input.summary: Greater Manchester goes to the polls on 30 July, but what powers does the region's mayor hold?
input.evidence_text: Andy Burnham's victory in the Makerfield by-election means he has to step down as Greater Manchester mayor Burnham has been mayor since the role was first created in 2017 and the race to become his successor will be fiercely contested by all involved But what is the role they will be fighting for? The role of mayor was created as part of a law passed in 2016, one of a number of big pieces of devolution legislation brought in by the Conservatives after taking power in 2010 In a 2012 referendum, Manchester rejected having a directly-elected city mayor, along with eight other major cities, but the decision to have a regional or metropolitan mayor was directly negotiated between the government and the 10 Greater Manchester councils Andy Burnham, who left Parliament to stand for the role, was chosen as Labour's candidate for the first mayoral election in 2017 and won, taking 63.4% of the vote

input.summary: Voters in all 10 boroughs will elect a new mayor to replace Andy Burnham on 30 July
input.evidence_text: People living in Greater Manchester will head to the polls in July to vote for who they think should be the region's mayor Residents living in all 10 boroughs will vote in the Greater Manchester mayoral by-election on 30 July We take a look at why it is happening, what the timetable is, and how the supplementary vote system works The election was triggered when Andy Burnham, who held the office for nine years, was elected the MP for Makerfield in last Thursday's parliamentary by-election By law, an MP cannot sit in the House of Commons and hold a metropolitan mayor role that has powers over local policing The Greater Manchester mayoral contest has to take place within 35 days of the post becoming vacant

input.summary: The large fire engulfed land near Dove Stone Reservoir on Saturday and has been burning ever since
input.evidence_text: Firefighters have said it could take days to extinguish a large fire on moorland in Greater Manchester, which broke out on Saturday Officers said the blaze at Dove Stone Reservoir in Greenfield, Saddleworth, had now merged with one several miles away at Tintwistle Moor near Glossop Clive Stanbrook, from Derbyshire Fire and Rescue Service (DFRS), said it was spreading because of the tinder-dry conditions and the "ever changing winds" He said crews were "doing all we possibly can to try and stop this fire from spreading any further", including dropping water from helicopters and carrying out reconnaissance with drones and aircraft. A woman, 20, has appeared in court charged with arson The blaze on Tintwistle Moor in Glossop started on 24 June and was declared a major incident on Sunday , with DFRS saying firefighters were facing "challenging conditions" Stanbrook said: "We've had a couple of helicopters dropping water as well, but also doing reconnaissance

input.summary: A Cheshire railway station is playfully renamed with a nod to England football anthem Three Lions
input.evidence_text: A railway station has undergone a patriotic rebranding ahead of England's World Cup semi-final game later Commuters pulling in at Holmes Chapel, Cheshire, will see new signage declaring: "It's Coming Holmes Chapel" The playful rebrand by rail operator Northern comes hours ahead of England's match against Argentina - the result of which will determine whether the Three Lions secure a spot in Sunday's World Cup final Northern's Alex Hornby said they wanted to give customers "something to smile about on the way to wherever they're watching the game" "Come on England," he said, adding: "It's coming Holmes Chapel." title: Shirt of England legend Roger Hunt up for auction in Wiltshire

input.lead: The striker wore the long-sleeved white shirt in a World Cup group game where he scored two goals
input.summary: The striker wore the long-sleeved white shirt in a World Cup group game where he scored two goals
input.evidence_text: A shirt worn by Roger Hunt during England's 1966 World Cup campaign could fetch up to £8,000 when it is sold at auction The former Liverpool striker wore the long-sleeved white shirt against France in a group game on July 21 where he scored both goals in a 2-0 win Hunt was part of the team that later lifted the World Cup when England beat West Germany 4-2 at Wembley Stadium The signed shirt will go under the hammer at Henry Aldridge & Son auctioneers in Devizes, Wiltshire, on Saturday. Auctioneer Andrew Aldridge said he was "delighted" to offer the lot "in this most important of World Cup weeks where England could end 60 years of hurt." "We were all terrified, not just Benji, I was terrified for my life as well," she said. She wants a legal change so all dog-on-dog attacks are taken seriously Lancashire Police said it had sought expert advice but the incident "did not meet the criteria" under the Dangerous Dogs Act Under the Dangerous Dogs Act 1991, , external dog attacks on other animals may amount to an offence, but it is for the individual police force and Crown Prosecution Service, to decide whether this is appropriate, on a case-by-case basis "I saw Benji in my arms then just looking up at me like 'just help me Mum'," Karen recalled to BBC North West Tonight

input.evidence_text: Courts 'Controlling' husband 'kerb-crawled' as wife walked to work before fatal attack, trial hears Syed Ahmad denies murder News Amy Walker Court reporter 17:26, 16 Jul 2026 Updated 23:25, 16 Jul 2026 View 2 Images The scene in Wythenshawe (Image: Ja A 'controlling' husband accused of murdering his wife would 'kerb-crawl' as she walked to work, her former colleague has told a trial Syed Ahmad, 45, is charged with the murder of his wife Anam Rafay on January 27 this year. He denies the charge and is on trial at Manchester Crown Court Prosecutors allege that Mr Ahmad had been 'controlling, abusive, and a bad husband' towards Mrs Rafay before she died. The couple were married by way of arranged marriage in 2005 when she was 16 years old In June last year, Mrs Rafay began speaking to a man she had met on TikTok, who posted under an account called Haroon Khan Travel, jurors previously heard It was said that Mrs Rafay went on to 'fall in love with him and wanted to marry him'. In September last year she contacted a mufti, also known as a divorce mediator, in Longsight to ask about obtaining a divorce application, jurors heard

title: Warning to households in this Greater Manchester borough named among 'UK's worst' for invasive plants

input.lead: Property News Warning to households in this Greater Manchester borough named among 'UK's worst' for invasive plants Japanese knotweed has been spotted 4,071 times across Greater Manchester News Phoebe Jobling Property Writer 17:12, 16 Jul 2026 View 3 A warning has been issued to
input.summary: Property News Warning to households in this Greater Manchester borough named among 'UK's worst' for invasive plants Japanese knotweed has been spotted 4,071 times across Greater Manchester News Phoebe Jobling Property Writer 17:12, 16 Jul 2026 View 3 A warning has been issued to
input.evidence_text: Property News Warning to households in this Greater Manchester borough named among 'UK's worst' for invasive plants Japanese knotweed has been spotted 4,071 times across Greater Manchester News Phoebe Jobling Property Writer 17:12, 16 Jul 2026 View 3 A warning has been issued to anyone living in this one Greater Manchester borough after it has been named amongst the 'UK's worst' areas for invasive plants Property specialists SAM Conveyancing examined records of 20 of the most common invasive plants to find where homeowners are most at risk. Each local authority was ranked by the number of recorded invasive plants per km² of land, measuring how densely the species are packed into The research revealed that Bolton is Greater Manchester's invasive plant capital, and is also the second most affected area in England, Wales and Scotland, ranking second of 349 local authorities Bolton , with 51.4 invasive plants per km², came second to Chesterfield nationally. Himalayan balsam is the greatest threat here, with a huge 3,994 records, followed by Japanese knotweed (1,805) and giant hogweed (981), with a total of 7,196 occurrences for the borough Next in Greater Manchester, and at 26th place nationally, comes Bury with 18.1 occurrences per km², where Himalayan balsam again leads with 876 records

Comments News Edward Barnes Local Democracy Reporter 17:05, 16 Jul 2026 View I Oldham Council still has no leader after another chaotic meeting saw claims Labour and Reform are ‘in cahoots’ and pleas to come together because people are ‘laughing at us’ After more than two months with no one in charge, the local authority is still in limbo with officers continuing to use emergency powers just to keep services running. Key political decisions cannot be made and multiple meetings have been cancelled Despite Reform gains, the recent local elections in May left no party with a majority or even a third of seats in the council chamber. Four times councillors have met to vote on a leader but the latest meeting on July 15 saw no one break through the stalemate On July 2, a legal letter was sent out by the council’s top legal boss Alex Bougetaf who said the government’s advice is ‘clear’ – that the current situation does not comply with the law – adding: ‘The current position is not sustainable beyond the very short term, and councillor Mr Bougatef warned if no leader was elected, he would then look to prepare a Section 5 report, which is produced when a council monitoring officer believes the council has or is about to break the law

input.evidence_text: Salford The plan that will shape Salford's future - full details of changes in each neighbourhood Almost 35,000 extra homes are planned for the city News Charlotte Hall Local Democracy Reporter 16:55, 16 Jul 2026 View 6 Images A site in Eccles town ce The masterplan that will shape Salford’s future has been signed off, with huge changes in store for neighbourhoods across the city It will include almost 35,000 new homes and the equivalent of 120 football pitches in fresh employment space by 2043 Council cabinet members approved the area’s Local Plan this week. It sets out the kind of developments the town hall wants to see in different parts of the borough - from infrastructure improvements to new parks and entire neighbourhoods Bosses intended to build at least 34,818 new homes in the next 17 years, in line with projected housing needs. They also want to see at least 268,000 square metres of new office floorspace and at least 584,000 sqm of new industrial and warehouse space Hundreds of homes are planned in Boothstown around the RHS Garden Bridgewater. input.lead: Fires Fire service issues Tintwistle update as eerie photos show smoke-covered moorland Derbyshire Fire and Rescue Service shared an update on wildfire near Glossop with people urged to stay away News Ashlie Blakey Live and breaking news reporter 12:3 New eerie pictures show crew
input.summary: Fires Fire service issues Tintwistle update as eerie photos show smoke-covered moorland Derbyshire Fire and Rescue Service shared an update on wildfire near Glossop with people urged to stay away News Ashlie Blakey Live and breaking news reporter 12:3 New eerie pictures show crew
input.evidence_text: Fires Fire service issues Tintwistle update as eerie photos show smoke-covered moorland Derbyshire Fire and Rescue Service shared an update on wildfire near Glossop with people urged to stay away News Ashlie Blakey Live and breaking news reporter 12:3 New eerie pictures show crews battling a huge wildfire in smoke-covered moorland at Tintwistle as the blaze rages on The photos show masked firefighters and crews tackling hotspots on the moorland near Glossop. The area is blanketed in thick smoke with the features of the crew lost in the hazy scenes Derbyshire Fire and Rescue Service (DFRS) shared the pictures alongside an update on the fire on Thursday (July 16). The service said around 80 firefighters worked on the scene on Wednesday with crews working to 'contain and supress' the fire today The wildfire at Tintwistle, above Woodhead Road in Glossop, first broke out nearly three weeks ago . DFRS declared a major incident as it spread over the moorland and burned an area the size of at least 350 football pitches The blaze, which is burning at the same time as another major fire near Dovestone Reservoir , has sent huge plumes of smoke across our region and beyond. The main focus of firefighting operations today is at Arnfield Moor, the fire service said

Katie Allen-Gyves was 38 and the Special Educational Needs Coordinator (SENCO) at Cedar Mount Academy in Gorton Senior coroner Alison Mutch, sitting at Stockport Coroners' Court, recorded a verdict of a 'tragic accidental death' and said Ms Allen-Gyves was 'under significant pressure at work and struggling to sleep' at the time. Ms Mutch said the mother-of-one died from the 'toxic effects' She said there was no evidence of any kind of any intention on her part to take her own life - saying she was planning for Christmas and wrapping presents The inquest heard Ms Allen-Gyves, from Sale , Trafford , had totally transformed her life after an addiction and was taking prescribed medication to treat opiod use disorder, as well as prescribed medication for ADHD. A 'significant level' of an over-the-counter medication was al Ms Mutch said it was likely Ms Allen-Gyves, who also taught geography at the school and was a member of its senior leadership team, was trying to 'manage down' her prescription at the same time as 'self-medicating' to try to sleep

input.summary: Property News Warning to households in this Greater Manchester borough named among 'UK's worst' for invasive plants Japanese knotweed has been spotted 4,071 times across Greater Manchester News Phoebe Jobling Property Writer 17:12, 16 Jul 2026 View 3 A warning has been issued to
input.evidence_text: Property News Warning to households in this Greater Manchester borough named among 'UK's worst' for invasive plants Japanese knotweed has been spotted 4,071 times across Greater Manchester News Phoebe Jobling Property Writer 17:12, 16 Jul 2026 View 3 A warning has been issued to anyone living in this one Greater Manchester borough after it has been named amongst the 'UK's worst' areas for invasive plants Property specialists SAM Conveyancing examined records of 20 of the most common invasive plants to find where homeowners are most at risk. Each local authority was ranked by the number of recorded invasive plants per km² of land, measuring how densely the species are packed into The research revealed that Bolton is Greater Manchester's invasive plant capital, and is also the second most affected area in England, Wales and Scotland, ranking second of 349 local authorities Bolton , with 51.4 invasive plants per km², came second to Chesterfield nationally. Himalayan balsam is the greatest threat here, with a huge 3,994 records, followed by Japanese knotweed (1,805) and giant hogweed (981), with a total of 7,196 occurrences for the borough Next in Greater Manchester, and at 26th place nationally, comes Bury with 18.1 occurrences per km², where Himalayan balsam again leads with 876 records

input.evidence_text: Politics Salford suburb 'at the point of collapse' where M60 traffic backs right up into housing estates Almost 2,500 residents of Boothstown lodged objections to the plans over concerns local infrastructure is already ‘at the point of collapse’ Comme A heated planning meeting ended in a frustrated sigh after almost 300 homes were greenlit on a plot of former greenbelt land in Salford . Dozens of residents showed up to the almost four-hour meeting to oppose the plans, which involve building 282 new homes off Leigh Road in Boot Peel Land, the developers, now have outline permission for the ‘new neighbourhood’, which includes ‘high quality family homes’ arranged around a new road layout, alongside a number of public green spaces and playgrounds Once greenbelt land, the plot was ‘deallocated’ through Places for Everyone, a scheme to create tens of thousands of new homes across nine of ten Greater Manchester boroughs But almost 2,500 residents lodged objections to the plans over concerns local infrastructure is already ‘at the point of collapse’ View 5 Images Kim Law, resident and chair of a local primary school added: “I just want to implore this committee to listen to the voices of ordinary people and families and not to the voices of big businesses.”

input.summary: Fires Smoke billows over Greater Manchester from moor fires LIVE Updated 35 mins ago Dovestone and Tintwistle moor fires LIVE as firefighters battle NEW blaze The moorland fires are continuing to rage on after woman appeared in court in connection wit Two major incidents have bee
input.evidence_text: Fires Smoke billows over Greater Manchester from moor fires LIVE Updated 35 mins ago Dovestone and Tintwistle moor fires LIVE as firefighters battle NEW blaze The moorland fires are continuing to rage on after woman appeared in court in connection wit Two major incidents have been declared amid the fires at Dovestone and Swineshaw Reservoir, in Greenfield in Oldham, and at Tintwistle Moor near Glossop. Dozens of firefighters remain at both scenes as smoke continues to billow into the air over our region And now, yet another moor fire has broken out following a car crash on the A628 Woodhead Pass On Thursday night, National Highways confirmed that firefighters are in attendance on the A628 Woodhead Pass between the A57 (Hollingworth) and A616 (Flouch), with the road shut in both directions. Images show a car engulfed in flames following the crash, which is then believed t It comes amid two major moorland fires. In Tintwistle, above Woodhead Road in Glossop, a blaze first broke out nearly three weeks ago . It has it spread over the moorland and burned an area the size of at least 350 football pitches Around 80 firefighters were working on the scene, the fire service said in an update yesterday (Thursday). Crews are continuing to 'contain and supress' the blaze and asked the public to avoid the area and stay off the moorland

input.evidence_text: Property News Warning to households in this Greater Manchester borough named among 'UK's worst' for invasive plants Japanese knotweed has been spotted 4,071 times across Greater Manchester News Phoebe Jobling Property Writer 17:12, 16 Jul 2026 View 3 A warning has been issued to anyone living in this one Greater Manchester borough after it has been named amongst the 'UK's worst' areas for invasive plants Property specialists SAM Conveyancing examined records of 20 of the most common invasive plants to find where homeowners are most at risk. Each local authority was ranked by the number of recorded invasive plants per km² of land, measuring how densely the species are packed into The research revealed that Bolton is Greater Manchester's invasive plant capital, and is also the second most affected area in England, Wales and Scotland, ranking second of 349 local authorities Bolton , with 51.4 invasive plants per km², came second to Chesterfield nationally. Himalayan balsam is the greatest threat here, with a huge 3,994 records, followed by Japanese knotweed (1,805) and giant hogweed (981), with a total of 7,196 occurrences for the borough Next in Greater Manchester, and at 26th place nationally, comes Bury with 18.1 occurrences per km², where Himalayan balsam again leads with 876 records

The service said around 80 firefighters worked on the scene on Wednesday with crews working to 'contain and supress' the fire today The wildfire at Tintwistle, above Woodhead Road in Glossop, first broke out nearly three weeks ago . DFRS declared a major incident as it spread over the moorland and burned an area the size of at least 350 football pitches The blaze, which is burning at the same time as another major fire near Dovestone Reservoir , has sent huge plumes of smoke across our region and beyond. He was released earlier this month after serving 14 years The 73-year-old has been stripped of his British citizenship leaving him with only Pakistani citizenship. However, he cannot currently be deported due to a 1971 law that forbids the removal of a small group of Commonwealth citizens who arrived in the UK more than 50 years ago The government has since announced plans to change the law in order to facilitate his removal from the country. Dozens of firefighters remain at both scenes as smoke continues to billow into the air over our region On Wednesday, Paul Fearnhead, Incident Commander at the Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service (GMFRS), described the area near Dovestone as 'really, really challenging' as the 'extensive wildfire' continued He said: "Currently we have around just short of ten fire engines, including our wildfire capabilities at this scene and they’re deployed across the moorlands in this warm weather , which is being hampered by smoke and wind "If you're a member of the public in the area, we are asking you to stay away. It's a small area for us to work in, and the moors at this time are very smoky, and we'd ask you not to come across this area." Both fires are sending huge plumes of smoke across our region and beyond - with the latest air quality maps today (July 16) showing the ongoing impact the fires are having across Greater Manchester

title: Man threatened to bomb town hall and accused Andy Burnham of 'protecting paedophiles', court hears

input.lead: Friday 10 July 2026 at 6:00pm Anthony McGrath accused Andy Burnham of protecting peodophiles.
input.summary: Friday 10 July 2026 at 6:00pm Anthony McGrath accused Andy Burnham of protecting peodophiles. A bomb hoaxer claimed he would target a town hall after accusing Andy Burnham of “protecting paedophiles" and threatening MPs, a court has heard Anthony McGrath, 57, claimed the bombs wo
input.evidence_text: Friday 10 July 2026 at 6:00pm Anthony McGrath accused Andy Burnham of protecting peodophiles. A bomb hoaxer claimed he would target a town hall after accusing Andy Burnham of “protecting paedophiles" and threatening MPs, a court has heard Anthony McGrath, 57, claimed the bombs would arrive from Russia and added: “Stop protecting #paedophiles @AndyBurnhamGM because the town hall will also be removed.” Posting on X in November 2025, McGrath also threatened Greater Manchester Police headquarters unless they returned property belonging to him, Liverpool Crown Court heard on Friday 10 July In January and February 2024 he sent a series of threatening emails to professionals, including Salford MP Rebecca Long-Bailey and Sir Robert Buckland, who at the time was MP for South Swindon, as well as a judge, solicitor, chartered surveyor and Nat West bank strategist McGrath, of Sale, Greater Manchester, was given a community order after the court heard he was suffering with his mental health at the time and had been sectioned following his arrest Martyn Walsh, prosecuting, said in one email sent to Ms Long-Bailey, McGrath said: “You all messed with the wrong person. My army is much bigger considering I have Russian and Chinese support. We will be armed and shoot to kill.”

Credit: Google Earth The council has recommended that councillors approve plans from Taylor Wimpey and Jones Homes for 168 properties on safeguarded land despite the project conflicting with local planning policies The 22.2-acre site off Dean Row Road in Wilmslow is designated as safeguarded land in Cheshire East’s local plan, affording it certain protections within the plan period up to 2030 However, Cheshire East Council’s planning department has urged committee members to approve the plans, due in part to the fact the local authority cannot currently demonstrate the statutory five-year housing land supply position According to a report published in April, Cheshire East currently has land identified for the delivery of 8,780 homes, which equates to 3.3 years supply The lack of a demonstrable five-year housing land supply “tips the planning balance in favour of a grant of consent”, according to a planning report, meaning that the Planning Inspectorate is more likely to overturn a refusal

title: A dish best served cold: A TV historian sued us to reveal our sources - and lost

input.evidence_text: Laurence Westgaph waged a legal campaign to find out who had spoken to one of our reporters. A year later, we met him in court On the first day of the trial earlier this month, TV historian Laurence Westgaph was asked to turn to page 255 of the hearing bundle: the thick ringbinder of documents that chronicle a year-long legal battle that was now, finally, coming to a head in court Westgaph, a well-known figure in Liverpool who has presented history shows on the BBC, was asked to read a Facebook post he’d published about me in July last year. He began to read it out “I served the big busta, Mr Herrmann this week…” Our barrister, Claire Overman, stopped him. She hadn’t meant for him to read the post out loud. Everyone in court read the Facebook post in silence. “This is just the starter though,” Westgaph had written on top of an image of himself staring at the camera with his shirt unbutton This was the denouement of a tough hour-long cross-examination of Westgaph, in which it often looked like he was struggling to explain why he had initiated this lawsuit in the first place. Claire, representing Mill Media, asked him about the dish “best served cold”

title: Let's get metaphysical: could an ex-philosopher be the new Burnham?

input.lead: She was one of Greater Manchester's first Green councillors.
input.summary: She was one of Greater Manchester's first Green councillors. Now she has her eyes on a bigger prize
input.evidence_text: She was one of Greater Manchester's first Green councillors. Now she has her eyes on a bigger prize Before politics, one interest dominated the life of Geraldine Coggins, leader of the Trafford Greens, candidate for Greater Manchester mayor, and that was metaphysical nihilism. The now-politician harbours a lifelong love of philosophy, studying the subject up and down the UK and What is metaphysical nihilism? When I ask her, Coggins seems reluctant to discuss it, directing me instead towards the book’s synopsis. “The content is very abstract,” she tells me, “and doesn’t affect my worldview now.” Maybe Coggins is trying to draw a distinction between the r And what a time in the Green Party it’s been. When I meet her, drinking matcha outside a glass-fronted MediaCity cafe, she runs me through the last decade-and-a-half in her Irish lilt: a councillor for eight years, a group leader for four; becoming one of Trafford's first Green c Coggins possesses a demeanour at once warm and authoritative, and it’s this unlikely mix that I’m told helped her win the favour of Altrincham’s electorate in 2018, when, alongside councillor Daniel Jerrome, she made history Two seats were vacant in the ward that year, and the pair stood as the Green candidates. Jerrome recalls a febrile atmosphere on polling day, with neither opposition parties nor the press able to predict what was to come

Most wildfires start due to human activities and are a risk to communities, emergency services as well as increasing pre All smoke is potentially harmful to your health. These include a 10% concentration threshold within a 100-metre radius, measures to prevent single-family homes from being “sandwiched” between HMOs, and restrictions on creating three At the same time, the SPD seeks to address concerns associated with poorly located or over-concentrated HMOs, such as parking pressures, noise, waste management issues and the loss of balanced communities

input.summary: Bury Council has officially taken possession of the keys to the new Radcliffe Enterprise Centre, marking a major milestone in plans to boost business growth and create new opportunities in the heart of Radcliffe
input.evidence_text: Press release — 23 June 2026 16:27 Share Share this story Copy link Link copied! Download Bury Council has officially taken possession of the keys to the new Radcliffe Enterprise Centre, marking a major milestone in plans to boost business growth and create new opportunities in the heart of Radcliffe The centre will operate from the ground floor of the existing Radcliffe Library on Stand Lane, following a period of fit‑out works that are underway. Library services will continue to operate uninterrupted from the first floor until they relocate to the new £40m Radcliffe Hub, se Once complete and fully opened later this year, the Radcliffe Enterprise Centre will provide flexible workspaces, modern meeting rooms, and on‑site business support services. Designed to help small businesses start up and grow, it will offer a multi-functional environment for loc Councillor Charlotte Morris, Cabinet Member for Regeneration and Growth, said: “Today’s handover marks another important step in delivering our long‑term vision for Radcliffe. The Radcliffe Enterprise Centre will give local businesses the space, support and confidence they need t “By regenerating existing buildings and investing in high-quality business facilities, we’re turning investment into real places for our communities to live, work and spend time, creating a stronger local economy and helping ensure Radcliffe remains a place where people can build

title: Historical Plantation Lodges open to all for the very first time

input.lead: Plantation Lodges and Gates have opened following an extensive renovation period, funded by the National Lottery Heritage Fund and Wigan Council The iconic Grade II* listed Lodges and Gates, located on Wigan Lane at the edge of Haigh Woodland Park, have been fully restored over a
input.summary: Plantation Lodges and Gates have opened following an extensive renovation period, funded by the National Lottery Heritage Fund and Wigan Council The iconic Grade II* listed Lodges and Gates, located on Wigan Lane at the edge of Haigh Woodland Park, have been fully restored over a
input.evidence_text: Plantation Lodges and Gates have opened following an extensive renovation period, funded by the National Lottery Heritage Fund and Wigan Council The iconic Grade II* listed Lodges and Gates, located on Wigan Lane at the edge of Haigh Woodland Park, have been fully restored over an 18 month period by Wigan Council and were officially opened by Lisa Nandy MP, Secretary of state for culture, media and sport, on Friday, 3rd J Speaking at the event, Ms Nandy said: “This is Wigan, we are friendly, welcoming people who believe these assets are for us all forever. This is more than a beautiful restoration, it’s a bridge between our past, present and future. Places like Haigh bring us together.’ The opening was also attended by The Mayor of Wigan Borough, Councillor Anne Collins, and Councillor Nazia Rehman, Leader of Wigan Council Also in attendance was Councillor Chris Ready, portfolio holder for communities and neighbourhoods. Councillor Ready said: "It’s incredible to have Plantation Gates and Lodges restored and open to residents for the very first time. The reaction to the transformation and the artwo Also in attendance was David Woods, a former resident who lived in the Lodges with his family over 50 years ago. He worked with local artists Leo Mercer and James Hooton, with his experience and memories forming a key part of 'What the walls remember' . This is an immersive art i

title: Wigan's new market to open in spring 2027

input.lead: Wigan’s new market will officially open next Spring following engagement with traders Wigan Council met with trader representatives yesterday (Tuesday 7 July) to provide an update on the new market, including how it will operate once open Wigan Market at Fettlers will open in Spr
input.summary: Wigan’s new market will officially open next Spring following engagement with traders Wigan Council met with trader representatives yesterday (Tuesday 7 July) to provide an update on the new market, including how it will operate once open Wigan Market at Fettlers will open in Spr
input.evidence_text: Wigan’s new market will officially open next Spring following engagement with traders Wigan Council met with trader representatives yesterday (Tuesday 7 July) to provide an update on the new market, including how it will operate once open Wigan Market at Fettlers will open in Spring 2027, allowing time for the council and traders to shape a venue that supports existing businesses and offers the best possible visitor experience in the town centre. The timetable also means traders will have the opportunity to enjoy Following the decision for the market to be operated directly by Wigan Council, additional work is now being undertaken to focus on developing an operating model that works for the people at the heart of the market This insight is now feeding into the final layout of the building’s internal design, ensuring the market’s long-standing businesses remain at the heart of one of the borough’s most significant regeneration projects Wigan Market forms the centrepiece of the wider Fettlers development and will bring the market back to the heart of Wigan town centre, creating a vibrant destination where people can shop, eat, meet and spend time together

title: Encouraging decline in youth smoking and vaping across Wigan Borough

input.lead: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally a
input.summary: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally a
input.evidence_text: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally and protect the health of our children and young people With more than 1,000 school children aged 14-17 included in the Trading Standards survey, the findings show the number of young people who have tried vaping dropped from around 44 per cent in 2023 to 18 per cent in 2026 The survey, carried out between November 2025 and February 2026, also highlights that smoking is at an all-time low, with around 97 per cent reporting to have never tried smoking Councillor Keith Cunliffe, Deputy Leader of Wigan Council and cabinet portfolio holder for adult social care and health, said: “I’m really pleased to see smoking and vaping rates among young people in Wigan Borough continuing to fall “Reducing tobacco and nicotine use among young people is one of the most effective ways to prevent long-term ill health and reduce health inequalities in our communities

title: Pioneering crop scientist receives Blue Plaque

input.lead: The Mayor unveils the new Blue Plaque for Dr John Garton on Park Road, Golborne A renowned Wigan Borough scientist has received a heritage Blue Plaque outside his former home in Golborne Dr John Garton’s (1863–1922) pioneering work in crop manufacturing has had a lasting impact a
input.summary: The Mayor unveils the new Blue Plaque for Dr John Garton on Park Road, Golborne A renowned Wigan Borough scientist has received a heritage Blue Plaque outside his former home in Golborne Dr John Garton’s (1863–1922) pioneering work in crop manufacturing has had a lasting impact a
input.evidence_text: The Mayor unveils the new Blue Plaque for Dr John Garton on Park Road, Golborne A renowned Wigan Borough scientist has received a heritage Blue Plaque outside his former home in Golborne Dr John Garton’s (1863–1922) pioneering work in crop manufacturing has had a lasting impact across the globe The Blue Plaque was unveiled at a ceremony on Park Road, Golborne, attended by the Mayor of Wigan Borough Councillor Chris Ready, cabinet portfolio holder for communities and neighbourhoods, said: “Dr John Garton’s work has helped transform farming practices and improve sustainability, making him a significant figure not just locally, but across the world “The Blue Plaque scheme is designed to celebrate the impact residents of our borough have made and Dr Garton’s legacy and influence in the world of agriculture will live on.”

input.summary: Inhale Me, Exhale You By Kate Partington Tue 2 Jun 10:00 - Mon 28 Sep 22:00 Art Tue 2 Jun - Mon 28 Sep From 10:00 Tue 2 Jun - Mon 28 Sep From 10:00 Arches Windows No booking required Unspoken feelings and moments of desire You can see this artwork in HOME Arches Windows on Whitwo
input.evidence_text: Inhale Me, Exhale You By Kate Partington Tue 2 Jun 10:00 - Mon 28 Sep 22:00 Art Tue 2 Jun - Mon 28 Sep From 10:00 Tue 2 Jun - Mon 28 Sep From 10:00 Arches Windows No booking required Unspoken feelings and moments of desire You can see this artwork in HOME Arches Windows on Whitwo This piece belongs to a wider series called ‘Desire Me.’ It aims to translate unspoken feelings and moments of desire that occur between us The focus is on longing, on reaching out and on instances of hopeful touch that happen in the early stages of a relationship. It seeks to capture the essence of sensations you get in your stomach that are so hard to verbalise, but that we all know and feel.” Kate Partington’s practice explores the subtleties of touch, connection, and the spaces that exist between bodies She is a mixed media artist working across analogue and digital spaces, constructing images that hover between materiality and emotion Her work examines how intimacy can be both tender and elusive, how connection can be felt rather than seen

title: Marina Abramović: Balkan Erotic Epic | Aviva Studios

input.lead: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović .
input.summary: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance a
input.evidence_text: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance artist, Abramović’s transformative work has reached millions of people – from opening the Pyramid Stage at Glastonbury Festival t A four-hour ritual where ancient myth meets performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic explores the eroticism, spirituality and traditions of Abramović’s homeland through 13 visceral scenes Brought to life by a cast of over 70 performers, dancers, musicians and singers, the experience unfolds across Aviva Studios. Audiences are free to choose their own path – with pop-up encounters punctuating the action, bringing bodies together in dance, song and ritual Watch Fertility Rite , a fevered ritual where bodies writhe against the ground in a desperate call for fertility. Witness Massaging the Breast where women gesticulate over graves to awaken the earth. In Scaring the Gods , women bare themselves to the sky to banish storms. Listen Unafraid to push the boundaries of eroticism and performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic asks its audience a timeless question: What are our bodies truly for?

title: Manchester City Football Club | Tickets, Travel & Matchday at the Etihad

input.lead: Whether you're a lifelong supporter or just in town for a football-filled weekend, here's everything you need to know-from getting tickets and planning your travel to catching a match and going behind the scenes at the Etihad Stadium.
input.summary: Whether you're a lifelong supporter or just in town for a football-filled weekend, here's everything you need to know-from getting tickets and planning your travel to catching a match and going behind the scenes at the Etihad Stadium. Manchester City was founded in 1880, original
input.evidence_text: Whether you're a lifelong supporter or just in town for a football-filled weekend, here's everything you need to know-from getting tickets and planning your travel to catching a match and going behind the scenes at the Etihad Stadium. Manchester City was founded in 1880, originally known as St. Mark’s (West Gorton), they became Ardwick Association Football Club in 1887 and Manchester City in 1894. For much of the 20th century, the club experienced a mix of highs and lows, with memorable moments like their 1969 FA Cup win and 1970 European Cup Winners' Cup triumph. Fast-forward to recent years, and City has become one of the most successful clubs in modern football. The club is one of the most successful in English football, having won ten league titles, seven FA Cups, eight League Cups, seven FA Community Shields, one UEFA Champions League, one European Cup Winners' Cup, one UEFA Super Cup and one FIFA Club World Cup. Under manager Pep Guardiola, they’ve transformed into a global powerhouse. input.evidence_text: Sat 18th Jul, 2026 - Sun 19th Jul, 2026 - Torr Vale Mill Open Fire Dining Club with Porta Torr Vale Mill, Torrvale Rd, High Peak SK22 3HS Sat 18th Jul, 2026 - Sun 19th Jul, 2026 Go to Manchester's Finest X profile Porta is taking its relaxed Spanish cooking into the open air this July, joining resident chefs Tartuffe for the next Open Fire Dining Club at Torr Vale Mill Founded by brothers Ben and Joe Wright, Porta has built a loyal following across its tapas bars in Chester, Altrincham, Salford and West Didsbury. Its approach takes its cues from the neighbourhood bars of Spain, with short menus, carefully chosen wines and plates designed to kee For this one-off collaboration, Porta executive chef José Garzón and Tartuffe’s Gary Weir have created a 10-course menu built around Spanish flavours, seasonal produce and live-fire cooking Guests will start with a Carlingford oyster served with guindilla tximitxurri, followed by dishes including a gilda de pulpo with Gordal olive, pickled chilli and braised octopus, Cantabrian anchovy on toasted coca bread with smoked requesón, and Galician queen scallops with ajad There’ll also be leeks grilled over the Big Green Egg with romesco, oxtail with piquillo peppers, and Porta’s much-loved grilled chicken thighs served with mojo picón and smoked potato

title: Young Musician's Summer School

input.lead: We’re inviting young people to explore music for film and compose their own soundtrack this summer!
input.summary: We’re inviting young people to explore music for film and compose their own soundtrack this summer! […] The post Young Musician’s Summer School appeared first on Band on the Wall
input.evidence_text: Young Musician’s Summer School . Young Musician’s Summer School. . Book here . . . . . . . Friday | 03.07.2026 . . . World of Music . Share . . . . --> . . We’re inviting young people to explore music for film and compose their own soundtrack this summer! Whether your child already attends our Saturday Music Club , or just wants to try something new, our Young Musician’s Summer School is suitable for all abilities! . . . . . Across this 5 day course, we’ll be exploring music for film, and working on composing music for the silent film ‘A Trip to the Moon’. The course will conclude with a live performance soundtracking the film for family and friends. . . . . We welcome all abilities and instruments to take part in this course. You can bring your own instruments, but we have keyboards, guitars, bass, violins, drums and percussion on site which can be used if you don’t have an instrument.. . . Snacks and drinks provided, and pizzas on the final day. Please bring your own packed lunch to sessions Monday-Thursday.. . Dates: Monday 17th – Friday 21st August. Times: 10:00-16:30 Monday-Thursday, then a performance for friends and family on Friday at 13:00 (students will be expected to come in at 10:00 sharp on the Friday).. Ages: 12-18 years old. Cost: £150 for the full course. Bursaries are available for those on lower income – please email learning@bandonthewall.org to arrange this.. . Spaces are limited – Book here . . . Please email learning@bandonthewall.org if you have any questions about this course

input.summary: The Only King is acclaimed as the first original Cantonese biblical musical to present the story of the Book of 1 Samuel in its entirety. The production follows the life of King Saul, from his anointing and rise to the throne to his gradual downfall through pride and disobedience
input.evidence_text: The Only King is acclaimed as the first original Cantonese biblical musical to present the story of the Book of 1 Samuel in its entirety. The production follows the life of King Saul, from his anointing and rise to the throne to his gradual downfall through pride and disobedience First staged in 2005, The Only King enjoyed multiple sold-out performances in Hong Kong and is widely regarded as a landmark work in Chinese Christian musical theatre To mark the 20th anniversary of its premiere, The Only King was presented in London and Cambridge in January 2026, where it received enthusiastic responses and strong audience acclaim. Building on this success, the production will embark on a UK tour in September 2026, with perfo Performed in Cantonese with English subtitles Suitable for audiences aged 3 and above. Children under the age of 12 must be accompanied by an adult * These tickets include an exclusive souvenir

final_reason: Intra-batch topic duplicate — same story kept from stronger source.

title: Mike Rice

input.lead: RNCM | Live Nation in association with UTA | 2026-09-27 | tickets
input.summary: RNCM | Live Nation in association with UTA | 2026-09-27 | tickets
input.evidence_text: There’s a cruelty to Mike Rice, a wickedness. In this show Mike talks about doing psychedelics with his brother, an entanglement with a Spanish Sausage Dog named Nino, and many other such rotten adventures ‘ Ireland has yet another legendary comedian ‘ – Dan Nightingale Irish farmer’s son, and general Nasty Character Mike Rice is one of Ireland and the UK’s fastest rising comedy stars. Having started comedy in 2016 Mike has amassed a large online following from podcast and stand-up clips, regularly getting millions of views on various platforms Mike began stand-up comedy in Chicago, where within two years he was working regularly at the most prestigious clubs in the city, including The Laugh Factory. He has since returned home because his mother made him, and has been quickly rising in the Irish and European scene, play Mike brought his first solo show The Best Boy in Ireland to the Edinburgh Fringe Festival 2018, in what was a sold-out run, and performed that same show to sold-out rooms at the Perth Fringe Festival in 2019 As well as co-hosting Mike & Vittorio’s Guide to Parenting with Vittorio Angelone, and Big Mike and The Chief with Rob Moriarty, Mike has appeared multiple times on smash hit podcast Have a Word and released his first comedy special in October 2023

title: Спектакль "Скамейка"

input.lead: Друзья, а вы давно ощущали настоящую магию театра?
input.summary: Друзья, а вы давно ощущали настоящую магию театра? 29 ноября 2026 года в Logan Hall — легендарная «Скамейка» с Алексеем Паниным
input.evidence_text: Спектакль «Скамейка» в Лондоне | Алексей Панин | 29 ноября 2026 :: EventFirst Home double_arrow Events double_arrow Спектакль "Скамейка" On sale Спектакль "Скамейка" schedule 29 Nov 2026 Sunday, 19:00 location_on Logan Hall 20 Bedford Way, London WC1H 0AL balcony 6:00 pm gpp_maybe NO REFUNDS Buy On MAP Buy Tickets Logan Hall £30.00 Select Logan Hall £35.00 Select Logan Hall £40.00 Select Logan Hall £45.00 Select Logan Hall £50.00 Select Logan Hall £55.00 Select Logan Hall £60.00 Select Logan Hall £65.00 Sold Out Logan Hall £70.00 Select Logan Hall £80.00 Select Logan Hall £85.00 Sold Out Logan Hall £90.00 Select Друзья, а вы давно ощущали настоящую магию театра? 29 ноября 2026 года в Logan Hall — легендарная «Скамейка» с Алексеем Паниным. После аншлагов в Европе и США спектакль впервые приезжает в Лондон. 📍 University College London, Level 1, 20 Bedford Way, London WC1H 0AL 🕕 DOORS: 18:00 🕖 SHOW: 19:00 «Скамейка» — знаменитая пьеса, когда-то блистательно сыгранная Олегом Ефремовым и Татьяной Дорониной на сцене МХАТа, а затем покорившая театральные площадки по всему миру. Это история о мужчине и женщине. О случайной встрече, которая постепенно превращается в откровенный разговор — честный, смешной, ранимый и очень человеческий. О том моменте, когда между людьми исчезают роли и, наконец, становится невозможно притворяться. На сцене — Алексей Панин и Ольга Силина. Тонкая актерская игра, живая энергия и удивительно узнаваемая история, в которой каждый услышит что-то своё. “Скамейка” — спектакль, после которого хочется немного помолчать и еще долго думать о главном. Если вам не хватает настоящего театра — этот вечер нельзя пропустить. Количество билетов ограничено. Продолжительность спектакля — 2 часа с антрактом. settings Loading venue plan... shopping_cart Your tickets: 0

The panel b A key theme throughout the discussion was recognising the signals that it is time to move on from legacy software and to modernise tech stacks. Richard said the trigger often comes from customers rather than technology itself. At Lloyds Banking Group (LBG), a move to Google Cloud Mariam pointed to developer experience and security as critical drivers for change. Systems built on older frameworks, such as early Ruby on Rails stacks, can become difficult to maintain or secure over time. She explained, “You don’t want to be running systems with outdated pack Chris explained that with Street being a relatively new business, their challenge is different - they don’t have legacy tech but they are scaling at such a pace that growth is doubling or tripling every year. This means that early architectural decisions can be constraining, but Discussion touched on bringing stakeholders on board with any upgrades or transformation. Chris and Mariam both reinforced the need for a strong narrative, effectively ‘selling’ the journey to non-technical stakeholders and bringing people on the journey with you Richard talked about pace of delivery, and said: “If you haven’t made the right decisions, the pace of delivery will change down the line. input.lead: Bolton Car Boot Sale at Bolton Car Boot Sale
input.summary: Huge Sunday car boot at Bolton's Macron Stadium (BL6 6JW), Feb-Dec. 300+ stalls, £1 per car entry, catering, toilets and dog-friendly Event date: 2026-07-19 Venue: Bolton Car Boot Sale Price: £1 Booking: https://carbootdirectory.co.uk:2096/listings/bolton-car-boot/ Status: https:
input.evidence_text: Bolton Car Boot Sale | Bolton Sunday Car Boot Sale | Times, Prices & Info Skip to content 210+ UK car boot sales — find one near you this weekend Type your postcode or town to find sales near you Search car boot sales About This Car Boot Sale Bolton Car Boot Sale is one of the North West's biggest weekly boot sales, held in the hardstanding car park of the Macron Stadium (formerly Reebok), Burnden Way, Horwich, Bolton BL6 6JW. It runs every Sunday from February right through to December, drawing over 300 sellers and thousands of bargain hunters each week. Sellers can arrive and set up from 6:00 AM, with buyers welcomed in from 7:00 AM. Entry costs just £1 per car for buyers. Pitches for sellers are £14 for a car, £20 for a van and £3 for a trailer, with regular and corner pitches available on request. Bring exact change to keep the queue moving, and call organiser Andrew on 07470784644 for more details. Expect hundreds of stalls across the concrete hardstanding pitches, so it goes ahead in most weather. On-site you'll find a 5-star catering service, doughnuts and coffee, ice cream and desserts, a meat auction, fresh fruit and veg, plants, and retro sweet stalls. The sale is dog-friendly, has male and female toilets, ample parking, and kids' rides and bouncy castles to keep little ones entertained. Last confirmed open: 8 June 2026. The Argentine ace spent seven years in English football, winning the Premier League with both United and rivals Manchester City England and Argentina are set to face off in a World Cup semi-final on Wednesday, renewing a famous footballing rivalry that carries geopolitical undertones. The Falklands War was a 74-day conflict between the United Kingdom and Argentina in 1982 The conflict began after Argentina invaded the Falkland Islands, a British overseas territory that Argentina continues to claim sovereignty over and refers to as Malvinas. In total, 649 Argentine and 255 British military personnel died, along with three civilians In 2023, Tevez revealed that his uncle was among those called up to fight for Argentina and had survived the conflict. input.evidence_text: 8 ноября 2026 года в Indigo at The O2 состоится концерт GORAN BREGOVIC в Лондоне Имя Горана Бреговича давно стало символом целого музыкального мира. Его путь начался в многонациональном Сараеве, где восточные мотивы естественно переплетались с западными традициями, а каждая улица имела свой ритм. Именно эта атмосфера сформировала композитора, впоследствии воз Впоследствии музыка Бреговича вышла за рамки концертных площадок. Сотрудничество с режиссером Emir Kusturica подарило мировому кинематографу саундтреки, ставшие неотъемлемой частью фильмов Time of the Gypsies, Arizona Dream и Underground. В этих мелодиях живут Балканы — с их своб Популярность Бреговича родилась не только благодаря знаменитым композициям “Kalashnikov”, “Ederlezi”, “In the Death Car” или “Bella Ciao”. Его главный талант — превращать народные мотивы в современное искусство, не теряя их подлинной силы. Вместе с легендарным “Wedding and Funera Окунитесь в этот балканский водоворот прямо в самом сердце Лондона! Город, который веками принимал выступления и разные культуры со всех континентов, на один вечер станет местом встречи британской сдержанности и безудержной балканской страсти. input.evidence_text: Plans for more than 500 homes in Stockport village Stantec also plans shops and food and drink venues Economic Development Ed Barnes and Local Democracy Reporter 05:00, 17 Jul 2026 What the housing development in Woodford could look like (Image: Ma More than 500 new homes could be built on the edge of a Stockport village joining up with another major development next door. Between them, Woodford could see more than 1,300 homes built Plans have been put forward to build the new homes off Chester Road in Woodford. The development would include up to 423 new homes as well as an extra care facility for those over 55 with up to 72 beds Alongside this, there are plans for shops, food and drink venues up to 100 square metres, new health services, a nursery or day centre, as well as a new nature park Approximately 2,900 people live in Woodford according to the latest estimates for 2024 and in the 2021 Census, 858 households were recorded In total, 567 homes could be built if the plans are given the go ahead by Stockport Council. title: Manchester-Sheffield tunnel plans: How Norway-style transport vision could slash travel times between Yorkshire and the North West

input.lead: Economic Development Yorkshire & Humber Manchester-Sheffield tunnel plans: How Norway-style transport vision could slash travel times between Yorkshire and the North West Project backers say it would boost local economies and improve Peak District environment Economic Development
input.summary: Economic Development Yorkshire & Humber Manchester-Sheffield tunnel plans: How Norway-style transport vision could slash travel times between Yorkshire and the North West Project backers say it would boost local economies and improve Peak District environment Economic Development
input.evidence_text: Economic Development Yorkshire & Humber Manchester-Sheffield tunnel plans: How Norway-style transport vision could slash travel times between Yorkshire and the North West Project backers say it would boost local economies and improve Peak District environment Economic Development A 14-mile tunnel beneath the Peak District mountains could cut journey times between Manchester and Sheffield by 30 minutes, according to an ambitious proposal A Norway-inspired dual carriageway would see the snakes of traffic commuting between Manchester and Sheffield removed from the national park, and sent underground instead The Woodhead railway line between the major northern cities would also be restored under the plans. The fast line between the two major northern cities closed to the public in the 1970s and then entirely in 1981 This scheme, named 'Trans-Pennine Connect', aims to make transport between south Yorkshire and the north west better. By putting that traffic underground, they say they can hand the Peak District landscape back to nature This in turn, they say, could boost the productivity of the region and inject millions into the local economy. But the plan goes far beyond that Trafford's Wharfside Regeneration Masterplan promises around 15,000 new homes, 48,000 new jobs, a possible new train station, green spaces and places for a new community to come together to eat, drink, shop and play Council leader Tom Ross wants to transform the land around the Old Trafford football grounds into a place where new and existing residents and businesses can thrive. He has lauded the Wharfside regeneration masterplan a 'foundation' for 'future success' in Trafford He said: "What other local authority wouldn't tear their right arm off to have the opportunity to be able to create something like this? We will make Trafford Wharfside a world-class place to live, visit and work [...]

title: Manchester United confirms new stadium location and plans for 'world-class' arena

input.lead: Economic Development Manchester United confirms new stadium location and plans for 'world-class' arena New 100,000-seater home will be some 350 metres away from Old Trafford Economic Development Tyrone Marshall Senior Manchester United Football Writer 08:32, 10 Jul 2026 Updated 0
input.summary: Economic Development Manchester United confirms new stadium location and plans for 'world-class' arena New 100,000-seater home will be some 350 metres away from Old Trafford Economic Development Tyrone Marshall Senior Manchester United Football Writer 08:32, 10 Jul 2026 Updated 0
input.evidence_text: Economic Development Manchester United confirms new stadium location and plans for 'world-class' arena New 100,000-seater home will be some 350 metres away from Old Trafford Economic Development Tyrone Marshall Senior Manchester United Football Writer 08:32, 10 Jul 2026 Updated 0 Manchester United have confirmed the club's new 100,000-seater stadium will be built on land recently purchased from Indurent, situated roughly 350 metres from Old Trafford United confirmed the location for the new stadium on Thursday in the Wharfside Strategic Masterplan and while the stadium won't be built on land currently connected to Old Trafford, they insist it will honour the current ground's history and traditions The new ground will be the centrepiece of a Stadium District, which will be purpose-built for sport, entertainment and year-round activity, with the new stadium serving as the flagship landmark of the wider Trafford Wharfside development The masterplan sets out a bold vision for Trafford Wharfside, including new and improved public transport links, enhanced rail connectivity, and extensive walking and cycling infrastructure. The vision is for a diverse neighbourhood creating 48,000 local jobs and 15,000 new homes It is estimated it could offer a £7.3bn per year boost to the UK economy, reports the Manchester Evening News

input.evidence_text: Danish-born juice bar and coffee concept JOE & THE JUICE is bringing its signature juices, coffee, and cult-favourite sandwiches to Manchester this summer with the launch of Sunlight House The opening of Sunlight House marks a major expansion for the juice bar in Manchester, continuing its growth across the city following the launch of its Mosley Street store last month and ahead of a new Oxford Road opening later this summer To celebrate launch day on Friday 10th July at, JOE & THE JUICE will be treating the first 100 customers through the doors to a free juice Designed with JOE & THE JUICE’s modern Scandinavian heritage in mind, the store will feature the brand’s instantly recognisable open counter layout, minimalist interiors and natural materials. The space will also integrate the JOE app experience, allowing customers to pre-order, The Sunlight House menu will showcase JOE’s full range of freshly prepared, functional offerings.
